### Principles we'll follow

1. **80/20 Rule:** Most of your running (80%) should be easy; only 20% is hard.
2. **One quality session per week:** Tue = threshold/tempo/intervals; Thu = optional moderate; Sat = long run (easy unless specified).
3. **No more than 10% volume increase per week** to reduce injury risk.
4. **Listen to your body:** If something hurts, walk it off; we'll adjust next week.
5. **Long runs are about time on feet, not pace:** Stay aerobic and enjoy the distance.

---

## Training Zone Reference

| Zone | Feel | Pace (min/km) | Purpose | Est. % of weekly volume |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 🟢 **Easy** | Conversational, relaxed | [Pace range] | Build aerobic base | 70–80% |
| 🟡 **Steady** | Comfortably hard | [Pace range] | Moderate effort | 5–10% |
| 🟠 **Tempo/Threshold** | Hard but sustainable | [Pace range] | Build lactate threshold | 5–15% |
| 🔵 **Strides** | Fast bursts | [Pace range] | Neuromuscular coordination | 2–5% |

> 📈 **Note:** Paces will evolve as your fitness improves toward goal pace.
